sutta: once the exhausted 1 dwelt near viethali at the gable hall in mahavana now the exalted 1 robin himself before noon went to the house of ugga the vase alien householder and sat down on the seat ready made ready there and ugga the visalian householder approached the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 from the mouth of the exalted 1 have i heard this lord from his own mouth have i received this the giver of good things gains the good lord to me the gruel from salt flowers is good eating that the exalted 1 except some from me out of pity the exalted 1 accepted out of pity then he said and the uga said from the mouth of the exalted 1 if i heard this the giver of good things gains the good lord good is the flesh of pigs with plenty of jujube fruit good is the only tube-like vegetable good is a mess of rice cleaned of black grains served with assorted curries and condiments good muslins from banares good is a couch with a fleecy cover wooden woolen cloth or coverlet spread with rocks odd of dear skins with awnings over it and crimson cushions at either end and though we know lord that it is not suitable for the exalted 1 this handle wood plank of mine worth more than a 100 thousand yen let the exalted 1 accept it out of pity and the exalted 1 accepted each gift out of pity then the exalted 1 gave thanks to ugga the vaisarian with this benediction who gives the good shall gain the good he who to upright men gives willingly clothes bad food drink the needful requisites and what's and what's past is put aside and obsesses not the mind [Music] who so in our hands doth find a feel for gives wise man forgoing what is hard in giving thus the good shall gain the good and when the exhausted 1 had thus given thanks to ugga the visalian he rose from his seat and departed now in due course ugga the householder died and thereafter arose in a certain inferable world at that time the exalted 1 dwelt near savathi at jaita grove in anatha pindika spa and there when the night was far spent oga the deva in lovely radiance biting up the whole of jaita growth came and visited him and after saluting stood at 1 side and the exhorted 1 spoke to him standing there saying i hope ugga things are well with you as we hoped and he said to be sure lord things are as the exalted 1 hoped then the exalted 1 addressed this verse to uga the deva who gives the good shall gain the good who gives the best shall receive shall best receive again the choice the choice receive the chief the chief plays win who gives the best the choice the chief that man has honor and long life wherever he arise that's the end of the suta
commentary: in this utah what he said here is that if a person gives good things he receives good things in return but 1 reason i [Music] uh mentioned this with us because here you notice that this man gave the certain food to the buddha and 1 of them is the flesh of pigs with plenty of jujube fruit this is 1 of the many instances in the sutas and in the vinaya where it is shown that the buddha and his disciples were not vegetarians but there's only a type of there's a type of meat that the buddha said was allowable to buddhist and there's a type of meat that is not allowable and that is mentioned in the majima nikaya the buddha said the type of meat that is allowable should have 3 conditions you did not kill you did not ask somebody to kill that's the first condition the second 1 you did not witness the killing he did not see the killing as a second condition the third 1 you did not hear the animal cry as it was being killed with these 3 conditions you don't have the direct karma of killing so it is that kind of meat is allowable the other type where you kill or you ask somebody to kill or you saw the killing or you heard the animal cry as it was being killed then it is not allowable i won't go into the details of why the buddha allowed it perhaps later when we discuss some other sutas are also concerned with mean eating and then i will go into it so here sometimes the the 1 of the last items is mentioned here is a sandalwood clang that's worth more than a 100 thousand generally luxurious items and the buddha did not want to accept especially in his younger days the buddha was very ascetic even robes the buddha did not accept robes that were the cloth of which was given by lay people for about 20 or 25 years it seems that he did not accept such ropes they only went about picking up this discarded cloth and this sometimes is discarded cloth could be found in cemeteries what they call channels grounds where people used to throw these corpses you know they wrapped these corpses with claw and then they throw these corpses on these channel grounds so that they were the corpses were eaten up by animals so mounds we used to go to these channel grounds and pick up this discarded cloth and use them and because these cloths never broken up they had to be pieces of it had to be sewn to make a rope that's why the banks rope their mouth rope is uh it's like a patchwork like a field you know like a rice field with pieces of glassware together so we notice from the vinaya and the sutas as the buddha grew older he relaxed some of the rules you know okay like in this case there's a sandalwoodner that's worth a 100 thousand uh he accepted it out of pity for ooga because ugga wanted to get get some merit uh in the i think mahaparinibana it was mentioned when the buddha was about to pass away somebody presented him with a cloth with gold or something sworn on it or something and the buddha also accepted now the next utah is
